objective: Build pipelines and bring data to GCP — on-boarding data from MySQL source systems to GCP by building batch ingestion pipelines
business_context: Centralise MySQL operational data on GCP to enable analytics and reporting as part of the data platform modernisation programme
functional_requirements: Ingest batch data from MySQL source systems into GCP
Execute pipeline on an hourly schedule
Handle up to 10 GB of data per pipeline run
Complete each pipeline run end-to-end within less than 1 hour of trigger
Deploy and operate pipelines in the Dev environment
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline run completes end-to-end within less than 1 hour of trigger
Pipeline executes successfully on hourly schedule
Pipeline handles 10 GB data volume per run without failure
Pipeline runs successfully in the Dev environment
expected_outputs: GCP target tables or datasets populated with batch-ingested MySQL source data
Hourly pipeline execution logs confirming successful run completion

Business context, functional requirements, and acceptance criteria were absent from the ticket template and have been derived from explicit source fields (source system: MySQL, volume: 10 GB, frequency: hourly, latency: less than 1 hr, environment: Dev).   - Row counts in GCP target match Oracle source within acceptable tolerance
  - Pipeline runs successfully across Dev, QA, and Prod environments
